RIVERSIDE                                 F. C. BORTON (1877-1948)                         STOCKTON
TELEPHONE (916) 920-2812                  JAMES PETRINI (1897-1978)                        TELEPHONE (209) 627-5800
FAX (916) 920-1514                        HARRY M. CONRON (1907-1979)                      FAX (209) 627-4309
                                          KENNETH O. PINSENT (1923-1984)
SAN BERNARDINO
TELEPHONE (714) 381-0527                                                                   IN REPLY REFER
FAX (714) 381-0528                                                                         TO OUR FILE NO.

SAN DIEGO
TELEPHONE (619) 232-2424
FAX (619) 231-5784


                                          November 8, 1990
                                                                                           Bakersfield
                                                                                           49718\24882

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention:  Legal Department

            Re:  Possible Conflict of Interest

Gentlemen:

     We are requesting your opinion in a matter affecting one
of our clients.  Our client is a City Council member for the City
of Tehachapi, and also a 25% owner of the limited partner which
holds a 50% ownership interest in a land development company.  Our
client thus has a 12-1/2% ownership interest in the  land
development company.

     The City of Tehachapi is preparing to sell bonds to build
roads and sewers on property being developed by various builders,
including the land development company of which our client owns the
12-1/2% interest.  Our question is whether is sufficient for our
client to abstain from voting on the bond issue, or whether our
client must resign from the City Council altogether.

     If at all possible, we would like at least a tentative
opinion by November 16, 1990, since that is when the City Council
is due to vote on the bond issue.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          Stephen M. Dake

SMD\cc

                                         JAMES PETRINI (1897-1979)
SACRAMENTO                                HARRY H. CONRON (1897-1979)         VISALIA
TELEPHONE (916) 925-8818                  KENNETH D. KINGSLEY (1925-1984)     TELEPHONE (209) 627-1088
FAX (916) 925-8814                                                           FAX (209) 627-2383

SAN BERNARDINO
TELEPHONE (714) 888-6697
FAX (714) 888-6865                                                          IN REPLY REFER
                                                                            TO OUR FILE NO.
SAN DIEGO
TELEPHONE (619) 231-6666
FAX (619) 231-0754


                              November 13, 1990
                                                                            Bakersfield
                                                                            (97 13) 24662

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Legal Department

Gentlemen:

    We are requesting your opinion on the propriety of the
following matters concerning our client. Our client owns a 25
percent interest in a limited partnership of a land development
company. The limited partner owns 50 percent of the limited
partnership, and thus our client has a 12-1/2 percent interest in
the land development company. The city is about to recommend to
the voters that they approve a bond issue, three million dollars of
which will go to benefit property held by the land development
company.

    Our client is a member of the city council of that city.

    Is it sufficient for compliance with the Fair Political Practices
Act for our client to abstain from voting on any matter affecting
the bond issue, or does he need to resign from the city council
altogether? The city council is due to vote on this November 16,
and thus we request that you render an opinion as quickly as
possible.

    If you have any questions about this letter, please do
not hesitate to contact the undersigned.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [Signature]
                                        Stephen M. Dake

SMD:lf

Our client is a City Council member for the City of Tehachapi, and also a 25% owner of the limited partner which holds a 50% ownership interest in a land development company. Our client thus has a 12-1/2% ownership interest in the land development company. The City of Tehachapi is preparing to sell bonds to build roads and sewers on property being developed by various builders, including the land development company of which our client owns the 12-1/2% interest. Our question is whether it is sufficient for our client to abstain from voting on the bond issue, or whether our client must resign from the City Council altogether. If at all possible, we would like at least a tentative opinion by November 16, 1990, since that is when the City Council is due to vote on the bond issue. Very truly yours, Stephen M. Our client owns a 25 percent interest in a limited partner of a land development company.
